description: DRAFT, non-normative (RUN-ATTEST-03 B.2). A single-predicate anchor credential (RUN-ATTEST-02): issuer-signed (the issuer is the record's repo author — T-AT6.2), one predicate per record (bundles are presentation-side only, T-PA4.4), fresh per-mint nonce (T-PA2.2), standing only on a vetting-event antecedent (T-PA3.1). Substrate (ID numbers, card numbers) is unrepresentable: every field is a closed enum, fixed-size bytes, or date claim (T-AT6.1). No ordinal/primary/rank field exists anywhere (T-PA1.1). Supersede is the issuer's record op; confirmation/revocation reach verifiers through the issuer's published treeHead records and holder-stapled inclusion proofs (V5, RUN-ATTEST-04) — no status endpoint exists, and the verifier never contacts the issuer (T-A4.11).

predicate.description: Closed predicate vocabulary; adding one is a governed vocabulary change.

mintNonce.description: Fresh per mint; no two credentials share derivation state (T-PA2.1/2.2).

era.description: V5/V6 (RUN-ATTEST-04): the governance-era anchor this credential was minted or reissued under — an ERA FACT, never a status. Old-era credentials never expire; silence carries no penalty; membership is era-graded, meaningful but factual (T-A4.16). An era-reissue is holder-requested, chains the ORIGINAL vetting antecedent (no new vetting — also V8's free-reissue structural pin, T-A4.14), and supersedes the prior credential.

vetting.description: The vetting-event antecedent: must resolve to the SAME issuer's vetting fact naming the SAME subject, or the credential folds to pending (T-PA3.1).

processProvenance.description: Inseparable process metadata: no encoding of a credential exists without it — no bare 'over_18: true' (T-AT6.2).

method.description: Closed; no free-form field where substrate could hide (T-AT6.1).
